Fern Polrrrcnr- PRACTTcEs C ouvrrssroN
-128 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento. CA 95gl.t-2329
(916) 32?-5660 o Fax (916) 322_08S6
May 4,2009
C. April Boling
7185 Navajo Road, Suite p
San Diego, Califomia g}llg
Re
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-09-098
Dear Ms. Boling:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the major donor reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Bec"ause you seek general guidance and
have not provided the name of the person whose duties are in quertion,"we are proviaing
informal assistance, rather than formal advice. Informal assistance does not privide the
requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section g3l 14;
Regulation 18329(b)(Z) and (c)(3).)
Qr/ESTTON
Does a major donor who contributed $ 10,000 to a committee in the city of Chula vista
(the "city") on April l,2o0g,have a filing obligation in the city if the City is holding a special
election on May 5,2039,.and the major donor'icontribution did not relate to the measure on the
ballot in the special election?
CONCLUSION
Yes' Having contributed $10,000 to a city committee, the donor became a major donor
committee and, under Regulation L8247..5(b), u 'tity general purpose" committee, subject to all
9f th-e filing requirements of such committees. theselnclude pre-election reports, whether or not
the donor made contributions in connection with a particular election.
I Th" Pnlitital Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections g1000 through gl()14. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Rrlitical practices
commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through lrt997 of rirle 2 of thi California cnde otlRegulations. All
regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of thi California Code ot'Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. I-09-098
Page No. 2
You are a professional treasurer and are requesting advice on behalf of a client regarding
the application of Regulation 18247 .5 to this clienias a major donor. On April l, 2009, your
client contributed $10,000 to a committee in the City that ii a primarily fonned ballot measure
committee. The committee is circulating petitions to place a measure on an unidentified future
ballot' on May 5,2009, the City will hold a special eiection on a ballot measure. None of your
client's contributions have been or would be made in connection with the special election. your
client would also like to make a contribution in excess of $10,000 in anotnJrlurisdiction.
AIIALYSN
Section 82013 identifies three ways that a person qualifies as a committee. Section
82013(c) defines one type of committee as any person or combination of persons who makes
contributions totaling $ 10,000 or more. This type of committee is commonly referred to as a
major donor committee.
Section 82027.5(a) provides that all major donor committees are "general purpose
committees" and defines "general purpose committee" in terms of whether the committee is a
state, county, or city general purpose committee. Under Section B2OZ7.5(d). a committee to
support or oppose candidates or measures voted on in only one city is a "ciiy general purpose
committee." 
- 
' 
Je
Regulation 18247.5 interprets Section 82027 .5 by providing that a committee files its
campaign statements in a state, county, or city if more than 50 perint of the committee,s
activities are in that jurisdiction. In addition, Section g4ZOO(g)(2) requires that a city general
purpose committee that makes contributions or expenditures iotaling bsoo o. more duiing the
period covered by the statement must file pre-eleciion statements. Section g4ll5(e)requires that
such statements are to be filed with the clerk of the city.2
For purposes of calculating the 50 percent, Regulation 18247.5(c) sets forth different
rules for recipient committees and major donor "o.-itt""r. As a general rule, major donor
committees count contributions or expenditures made during the cunent calendar year at the end
of June and December, prior to their semi-annual filings. (Rigulation 1g247.5(c)(2).) However,
in this case the City is holding a special election on May 5, zoog. up to the closing date of the
pre-election reports due for the special election, the only contribution you. client has made has
been in the city' Here, the characterization of a committee as a state, county, or local committee
by calculating percentages of its contributions is made as of the close of a poientially relevant
filing period' Accordingly, your client is required to file pre-election campaign statements with
the City for the special election. Even if a second contribution has the effect if changing the
FACTS
' For a major d-onor committee. the pre-election statements are filed on Form 461 ..Independent
Expenditure and Major Donor Committee Campaign Statement."


Under Section 84215(9), a committee required to file campaign statements under section
84200'5 in places designated in subdivision (e) of Section 84215 must continue to file statements
in those places until the end of the calendar year. This requirement is reinforced by Regulation
18427.5(d), which states that "if, after filing reports with a local filing officer, a ciiy coirmittee
becomes a county or state committee, the committee must continue fiiing reports with the
original city through the end of the calendar year." Accordingly, once yo-u. "ti"nt qualified as a
city general purpose committee and became subject to the filft requirements of such a
committee, he or she must continue to file statements in the Ci[, uniit the end of the calendar
year.
File No. I-09-098
Page No. 3
character of the committee to a county or state committee, the donor must continue to file reports
in the City until the end of the calendar year
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
